content:
# Rock Mechanics

**Summary**: The study of the mechanical properties and behavior of rocks under stress.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] applied rock-mechanics to evaluate brittleness and frackability in shale.

- [[2017-tayyab|Tayyab, 2017]] evaluated rock mechanics through RQD measurements in boreholes.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] used rock mechanics to evaluate Young's modulus (Es, Ed) and Poisson's ratio.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used to evaluate the impact of chemical additives on rock hardness and durability.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] evaluated the impact of acid treatment on rock mechanics.

- [[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023]] studied rock mechanics to assess the impact of acid and CO2 on carbonate reservoirs.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] applied rock mechanics to evaluate wellbore stability and sand production.

## Key Subtopics

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] ['brittleness index', 'frackability', 'elastic constants']

- [[2017-tayyab|Tayyab, 2017]] evaluated rock mechanics through RQD measurements in boreholes.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] used rock mechanics to evaluate Young's modulus (Es, Ed) and Poisson's ratio.

- [[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022]] used to evaluate the impact of chemical additives on rock hardness and durability.

- [[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023]] evaluated the impact of acid treatment on rock mechanics.

- [[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023]] studied rock mechanics to assess the impact of acid and CO2 on carbonate reservoirs.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] applied rock mechanics to evaluate wellbore stability and sand production.

## Methods and Approaches Used

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] ['velocity-analysis', 'xrd']

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Used elastic constants (Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio) as primary indicators for brittle behavior. ([[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016, p. 56]])

- Borehole logs showed R.Q.D. values as low as 0% to 14% in several sections. ([[2017-tayyab|Tayyab, 2017, p. 126]])

- Young's modulus (E*) from impulse hammer provides a reliable proxy for rock strength. ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020, p. 31]])

- Chemical treatments significantly improved the indentation hardness of chalk and limestone. ([[2022-samarkin|Samarkin, 2022, p. 13]])

- Acidizing decreases the stiffness and elastic modulus of the rocks to various degrees. ([[2023-al-garni|Al-Garni, 2023, p. 2]])

- Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio were reduced after acid treatment in limestone. ([[2023-aldhafeeri|Aldhafeeri, 2023, p. 40]])

- Young's modulus is a key indicator for predicting unconfined strength. ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002, p. 40]])

## Competing Approaches and Debates

- [[2016-lawal|Lawal, 2016]] Defining a single standard for brittleness in heterogeneous shale.

## Open Questions and Research Gaps
